metadata.title: How to Make a Few MORE Billion Dollars: Brad Jacobs

conclusion: Jacobs built eight billion-dollar companies, raised ~$50 billion, integrates before close with same-day town halls for 3,600 people, and keeps org charts to one page. TM since 16 and Ellis REBT manage perfectionism; Apollo Ohno flagged him as a rare positively fueled founder. XPO/GXO investors track roll-up speed, 1–3x EBITDA leverage, and post-merger flattening.
background:
Senra reads Jacobs' follow-up after episode 335 — Jacobs made 'a few more billion' in two years. The book answers questions the first skipped: capital raising across public markets, family offices, pensions, hedge funds, and debt; mastering integration with same-day town halls; and mental models from Albert Ellis REBT, CBT, DBT, and mindfulness.

Jacobs started companies from zero to 150,000 employees, prefers public markets for non-dilutive access, and notes QXO shareholders found him via Senra's podcast ($750M from John Kugan). He flattens orgs to nine layers, eliminates 'manages managers' roles, and assigns integration line items with owners and dates. Stress is constant — he works seven days and would work an eighth.

Jacobs quotes Don Valentine on storytelling via Collateral; QXO profile drew $750 million from John Kugan after Senra episode. He targets 1–3x EBITDA debt, warns hedge funds lack patience, and assigns integration thousands of line items with owners and firm dates — fish-rots-at-head COO Wayan Hicks lens on acquired leaders.

Jacobs works seven days, would add eighth; Kelleher stress quote parallels his comfort with anxiety. Integration assigns thousands of line items with owners and dates; fish rots at head on acquired CEOs.
important_facts: Jacobs built eight separate billion-dollar companies over ~46 years as CEO; raised ~$50 billion total across M&A and organic growth — family offices, pensions, public equity, and debt at target 1–3x EBITDA leverage.

Integration playbook: negotiate unrestricted pre-close access; Beacon Buildings deal closed with Zoom town hall inviting 3,600 people within three hours of press release — majority attended; rip band-aid vs slow legacy branding. QXO drew $750M after Senra podcast; John Kugan endorsement became capital event.

Org design rule: charts must fit one page with minimal dotted lines; empty seat less damaging than wrong hire; quarterly employee surveys distill three questions — what's well, needs fixing, how improve company. TM twice daily since 16; Ellis REBT flexible preferences; Apollo Ohno noted positive fuel rarity.

mental_model.name: Positive Roll-Up Machine
mental_model.components: Think extraordinarily big day one; rearrange inner monologue via meditation and REBT; raise from aligned long-term capital; integrate fast with dashboards everyone reads; flatten hierarchy; assign integration owners with dates. Public markets keep management sharp every 90 days.
mental_model.application: XPO/GXO serial acquirers win when town halls and org clarity beat synergy slides. Jacobs' Ellis reframe — prefer excellence but accept error — sustains serial CEO stamina. Watch leverage at 1–3x and post-merger culture absorption. GXO spin benefits from same flatten-to-nine-layers doctrine; public scrutiny every 90 days is feature not bug for Jacobs' capital access.

key_insights.question: Why does Jacobs favor public markets?
key_insights.answer: Non-dilutive access without board seats or coupons; quarterly earnings discipline; retail and index investors as marketing. Family offices offer operator wisdom; pension money is sticky; hedge funds flip — he warns on misalignment. Pension and index money sticky; hedge funds flip — match investor to horizon.

key_insights.question: When does M&A integration start?
key_insights.answer: Before close with unrestricted access — town halls within hours, Q&A not lectures, intelligence dashboards for all levels. Band-aid rip on legacy tech and branding; lingering under old structures limits upside.

key_insights.question: How does Jacobs handle perfectionism?
key_insights.answer: TM since 16, twice-daily meditation, Ellis REBT demanding flexible preferences not musts — 'Okay, how upset were you?' Apollo Ohno noted Jacobs as rare positively fueled founder among eight billion-dollar outcomes. Meditation and REBT are CEO infrastructure for eight billion-dollar cycles.

top_investment_implications.thesis: Jacobs' $50B capital markets fluency and same-day integration playbook underpin XPO roll-up economics — watch leverage discipline and talent retention post-deal.
GXO inherits warehouse logistics spin-out quality — Jacobs' org-chart austerity and dashboard transparency are the operating lens, not a fresh long thesis from this episode alone.

golden_quotes: Eight separate billion-dollar companies — Jacobs as the positively fueled founder Apollo Ohno couldn't believe existed.
Raised ~$50 billion — public markets for discipline, pensions for sticky capital, debt at 1–3x EBITDA for sleep-at-night leverage.
An empty seat is less damaging than the wrong hire — org charts must fit one clean page.

events.event: Builds eight billion-dollar companies across industries.
First book How to Make a Few Billion Dollars covered.
QXO raises after Senra podcast exposure; John Kugan invests $750M.
Sequel More Billion Dollars published; Beacon integration case study.
